title: UnitedHealth Group Inc. (UNH) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of UnitedHealth Group Inc. (UNH). Economic Prospect Score: 72/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: UnitedHealth Group Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 10% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% WACC balances UnitedHealth Group Inc.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisTitle: UnitedHealth Group Inc.: A Fundamental Assessment
thesisParagraphs: UnitedHealth Group Inc. (UNH) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 10% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 9.0% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is UnitedHealth Group Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 10% FCF growth and 9.0% WACC, Opus calculates UNH's intrinsic value at approximately $637.54 per share, suggesting the stock is undervalued at $530.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive UNH's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (10%) and discount rate (9.0%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: UnitedHealth is the largest health insurer in the US with Optum creating a vertically integrated healthcare powerhouse. The combination of insurance + pharmacy benefits + care delivery + data analytics is unique. Growth is steady but faces political headwinds on healthcare costs and recent DOJ scrutiny.
prospectKeyRisks: Political and regulatory risk — both parties increasingly targeting healthcare costs, potentially capping margins or restricting vertical integration
DOJ antitrust investigation into Optum's relationship with UnitedHealthcare creating structural separation risk
Medical loss ratios increasing as post-COVID utilization normalizes and healthcare cost inflation accelerates
prospectKeyCatalysts: Optum Health expanding care delivery to become the largest employer of physicians in the US, deepening the value-based care model
AI-driven claims processing and clinical decision support reducing administrative costs by 15-20%
Medicare Advantage enrollment continuing to grow as the 65+ population expands, providing a decade-long demographic tailwind
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
